summary: Cloud storage photo testing completed. All 13 backend tests passed. Fixed frontend bug where photo IDs were used directly as img src instead of constructing proper /api/photos/serve/{photo_id} URLs. Photos now load correctly on profile page and header avatar.

tests: Photo upload to cloud storage (/api/photos/upload) - PASS
Clear and blurred photos have different MD5 hashes - PASS
User avatar_url updated after photo upload - PASS
Discovery endpoints return correct photo URLs with blur parameter - PASS
Legacy photo endpoint still works - PASS

frontend_photo_display.issue: ProfileTab.js and Layout.js were using raw photo IDs as img src instead of constructing proper URLs
frontend_photo_display.fix: Added getPhotoUrl() helper function to construct /api/photos/serve/{photo_id} URLs

critical_code_review_comments: Backend cloud storage implementation is correct - uploads both clear and blurred versions
Backend /api/photos/serve endpoint correctly returns blurred or clear version based on blur parameter
Discovery endpoints correctly add ?blur=true to avatar URLs for pre-reveal profiles
BlurredImage component correctly handles URL construction for discovery cards
FIXED: ProfileTab.js now uses getPhotoUrl() helper to construct proper URLs
FIXED: Layout.js header avatar now uses getPhotoUrl() helper
seed_data_creation: Test photos created during backend tests (auto-cleaned)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cloud storage photo system fully working. Photos are uploaded to Emergent Object Storage with both clear and blurred versions. The /api/photos/serve/{photo_id}?blur=true endpoint returns server-side blurred version. Discovery endpoints return avatar URLs with ?blur=true for pre-reveal profiles. Frontend ProfileTab.js and Layout.js were fixed to properly construct photo URLs from photo IDs.
